flags.concern: Selection bridge semantics: mirroring local selection with an effect that calls `clearGallerySelection()` on every local-empty transition is incompatible with the current peer-clear contract and will wipe the newly-selected owner. In this repo `selectGalleryItem()` / `selectGalleryItems()` call `peerClearRef.current?.()` before they replace the gallery map, and the existing video-editor peer callback intentionally uses a local-only clear (`clearTimelineOnly`) that does not touch gallery state. If the new timeline/batch bridge clears gallery state when peer-clear empties its local ids, a gallery click or another surface selection will boomerang back and clear the fresh attachments.
flags.evidence: Addressed three significant flags: (1) FLAG-001 — Added `isPeerClearingRef` guard to prevent boomerang clearing; the peer-clear callback now sets a ref flag before clearing local state, and the sync effect skips the gallery clear when this flag is set. Also added `deselectGalleryItems(currentIds)` in the peer-clear callback to remove bridge-owned items from the gallery map. (2) FLAG-002 — Documented that toggle/append in the gallery does not fire peer-clear (matching existing video-editor behavior) and added the `deselectGalleryItems` call in peer-clear to clean up bridge items when non-toggle selection happens. Reframed as a known design decision, not a bug. (3) FLAG-003 — Changed the batch editor bridge input from `selection.selectedIds` to `isMobile ? selection.mobileSelectedIds : selection.selectedIds` so mobile batch selections are also bridged to GallerySelectionContext. Added a boomerang-specific smoke test step.

flags.concern: Cross-surface exclusivity: the plan says the generations gallery needs no changes, but gallery multi-select paths do not invoke peer-clear today. `GenerationsPaneGallery` passes `toggle: modifierKeys.isMultiSelectModifier` for Cmd/Ctrl-click, and lasso selection can pass `{ append: true }`; `GallerySelectionContext` only calls `peerClearRef.current?.()` when `toggle`/`append` are false. After wiring timeline/batch selections into the gallery context, additive gallery selection will merge with the other surface instead of clearing it, so the stated mutual-exclusivity behavior is not actually guaranteed.

(2) FLAG-002 — Documented that toggle/append in the gallery does not fire peer-clear (matching existing video-editor behavior) and added the `deselectGalleryItems` call in peer-clear to clean up bridge items when non-toggle selection happens. flags.concern: Batch-selection plumbing: the planned batch bridge reads `selection.selectedIds`, but the mobile batch editor does not use that state. `useSelection()` stores mobile picks in `mobileSelectedIds` whenever `isMobile && generationMode !== 'timeline'`, and `BatchModeContent` always forces `generationMode='batch'` on mobile. Following the plan literally leaves mobile batch selections invisible to `GallerySelectionContext`, so AgentChat attachments still will not appear from that surface on mobile.
